title: Quiz: Noise and Loss in Quantum Channels

questions.question: What is the primary cause of exponential photon loss in optical fiber?
questions.explanation: Material absorption (silica absorbing photon energy) and Rayleigh scattering (density fluctuations in the glass) are the primary causes of fiber attenuation, leading to exponential photon loss with distance.

questions.question: What does T2 (dephasing time) characterize?

options.text: How fast a qubit loses energy
How fast quantum coherence (phase) is lost

questions.explanation: T2 characterizes how quickly phase coherence is lost—the off-diagonal elements of the density matrix decay. T2 includes contributions from both energy relaxation (T1) and pure dephasing.

questions.question: As fiber distance increases, what happens to the QBER contribution from dark counts?
questions.explanation: As distance increases, signal photons decrease exponentially while dark counts remain constant. When dark counts dominate, each detection is equally likely to be signal or noise, giving 50% error rate.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the decoy-state protocol in QKD?

options.text: To estimate single-photon contribution and detect PNS attacks

questions.explanation: Decoy states (varying intensity pulses) allow estimation of the single-photon yield, enabling detection of photon-number splitting (PNS) attacks that exploit multi-photon pulses in weak coherent sources.

questions.question: Which error correction code achieves efficiency closest to the Shannon limit?
questions.explanation: LDPC codes can achieve efficiency within 5% of the Shannon limit (efficiency ~1.05). CASCADE, the original QKD error correction, has efficiency around 1.16. Polar codes can be even closer to optimal.

questions.question: What technique converts a partially secure key into a shorter, fully secure key?
questions.explanation: Privacy amplification uses universal hash functions to extract a shorter key that is statistically independent of any information an eavesdropper might have. The key length is reduced by the amount of leaked information.

questions.question: A photon traveling through fiber experiences significant T1 relaxation (energy decay).
questions.explanation: Photons in fiber have extremely long coherence times (effectively infinite T1 and T2 for practical purposes). The main issue is photon loss, not decoherence. This is why photons are ideal carriers for quantum communication.
